Title: Cotton GhSSI2 isoforms from the stearoyl acyl carrier protein fatty acid desaturase family regulate Verticillium wilt resistance

Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Defense signaling associated with HR-like responses and fungal disease resistance
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Upregulated in GhSSI2-silenced plants and contributes to elevated SA accumulation via the PAL pathway
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: VIGS cosilencing with GhSSI2; RT-qPCR; PAL activity assay; SA/SAG quantification; expression after V. dahliae infection
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Induced in GhSSI2-silenced leaves and after V. dahliae infection
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. GhPAL1 transcript increased in GhSSI2-silenced cotton. 2. PAL pathway, rather than ICS1, was implicated as the major source of elevated SA in low-18:1 cotton. 3. Cosilencing GhPAL with GhSSI2 lowered SA but did not abolish H2O2 accumulation or PR induction, supporting SA-independent defense activation downstream of GhSSI2 loss.
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential auxiliary target for tuning SA accumulation, but alone is unlikely to explain the full resistance phenotype because defense activation remained after SA reduction
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Studied as part of a GhPAL set; individual accession not specified.

Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Defense signaling associated with HR-like responses and fungal disease resistance
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Upregulated in GhSSI2-silenced plants and contributes to SA accumulation through the PAL pathway
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: VIGS cosilencing with GhSSI2; RT-qPCR; PAL activity assay; SA/SAG quantification; expression after V. dahliae infection
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Induced in GhSSI2-silenced leaves and after V. dahliae infection
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. GhPAL2 was significantly induced in the low-18:1 GhSSI2-silenced background. 2. Along with GhPAL1, it supported PAL-mediated SA biosynthesis in cotton. 3. Reduced SA after GhPAL cosilencing did not remove defense outputs, indicating SA is not essential for GhSSI2-triggered resistance.
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Possible lever to modulate SA content, but not a standalone determinant of the GhSSI2 resistance phenotype
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: One of three selected GhPAL isoforms with high basal expression; individual accession not specified.

Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Defense signaling associated with pathogen response
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Not significantly altered in GhSSI2-silenced plants; included in PAL pathway testing
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: RT-qPCR; VIGS of GhPAL set; SA/SAG quantification
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Selected based on relatively high basal expression in transcriptome database
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. GhPAL3 was examined as a representative PAL family member. 2. Unlike GhPAL1/2, its transcript was not significantly induced by GhSSI2 silencing. 3. It was included in the broader evidence that the PAL pathway contributes to cotton SA synthesis.
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Lower priority target than GhPAL1/2 for manipulating SA in this context
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Included because of high basal expression; individual contribution unresolved.

Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Silencing GhICS1 reduced SAG and partially reduced SA-related pools, but GhICS1 expression was not induced in GhSSI2-silenced plants; therefore it is not the main route of SA increase in low-18:1 cotton
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: VIGS single and cosilencing with GhSSI2; RT-qPCR; SA/SAG quantification
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: No obvious expression change after GhSSI2 silencing or V. dahliae infection in the tested context
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. GhICS1 was tested as an alternative SA biosynthetic route. 2. Its transcript was not significantly induced in GhSSI2-silenced cotton. 3. Results supported PAL pathway predominance for SA synthesis in this system.
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Not a primary target for reproducing the GhSSI2-associated resistance phenotype
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Included to distinguish ICS- versus PAL-derived SA biosynthesis.

Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Stearoyl-acyl carrier protein desaturase family; FA_desaturase_2 domain (PF03405)
Pathway_Genes.Biosynthetic_Pathway: Fatty acid desaturation / unsaturated fatty acid biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Metabolic_Step_Position: upstream, committed control step for oleic acid pool
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Introduces a Δ9 double bond into stearoyl-ACP to convert 18:0 to 18:1
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Supported as a major key control step for the cotton 18:1 pool
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Membrane lipid component and defense-related lipid signal influencing HR-like responses and resistance to vascular fungal pathogens
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Silencing reduced 18:1 and increased 18:0, triggered HR-like lesions, elevated SA and NO, altered JA responsiveness, and enhanced resistance to Verticillium dahliae and Fusarium oxysporum
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Group-specific VIGS; fatty acid profiling by GC; disease assays; fungal recovery assay; RT-qPCR; hormone quantification; histochemical staining
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Expression was significantly suppressed after V. dahliae inoculation in resistant cultivar ND601
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: V. dahliae infection; F. oxysporum infection; exogenous glycerol; MeJA treatment
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. GhSSI2-A belongs to the GhSSI2 group that dominantly maintains the cotton oleic acid pool. 2. Silencing the group lowered 18:1 and caused broad defense activation with HR-like cell death. 3. Low 18:1 enhanced resistance to Verticillium wilt and Fusarium wilt.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Candidate target for engineering fungal disease resistance via partial downregulation or fine-tuned editing of oleic acid homeostasis; caution is needed because strong suppression causes lesion mimic and growth-defense tradeoffs
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Leaf curling, spontaneous necrotic lesions, HR-like phenotype, possible fitness penalty from constitutive defense activation
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: One of three redundant/dominant GhSSI2 isoforms; study mainly assessed the group rather than isoform-specific biochemical separation.

Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Stearoyl-acyl carrier protein desaturase family; FA_desaturase_2 domain (PF03405)
Pathway_Genes.Biosynthetic_Pathway: Fatty acid desaturation / unsaturated fatty acid biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Metabolic_Step_Position: upstream, committed control step for oleic acid pool
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Introduces a Δ9 double bond into stearoyl-ACP to generate oleic acid
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Supported as part of the dominant GhSSI2 control module for the cotton 18:1 pool
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: As part of the GhSSI2 group, contributes to maintaining 18:1 levels; group silencing enhances fungal resistance and activates defense outputs
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Group-specific VIGS; fatty acid profiling; disease assays; hormone and ROS measurements
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Part of GhSSI2 transcript set repressed after V. dahliae inoculation
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: V. dahliae infection; F. oxysporum infection; glycerol treatment
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. GhSSI2-B is a member of the dominant cotton SSI2 isoform group. 2. The GhSSI2 group controls oleic acid abundance and associated defense phenotypes. 3. Reduced GhSSI2 activity shifts metabolism and immunity toward resistance against vascular fungi.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful component for allele mining or multiplex editing of the GhSSI2 locus set to enhance resistance while avoiding severe lesion mimic
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Constitutive defense activation and lesion-like symptoms when the whole group is strongly suppressed
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Function inferred from group-level silencing rather than uniquely isolated isoform knockout.

Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Stearoyl-acyl carrier protein desaturase family; FA_desaturase_2 domain (PF03405)
Pathway_Genes.Biosynthetic_Pathway: Fatty acid desaturation / unsaturated fatty acid biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Metabolic_Step_Position: upstream, committed control step for oleic acid pool
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Catalyzes desaturation of stearoyl-ACP to oleoyl-ACP in plastids
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Supported as part of the dominant GhSSI2 module controlling 18:1 content
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Membrane and signaling fatty acid affecting immunity
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Contributes within the GhSSI2 group to sustaining oleic acid; group suppression reduces 18:1 and enhances Verticillium/Fusarium resistance
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Group-level VIGS; GC fatty acid analysis; disease phenotyping; hormone/ROS assays
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Part of GhSSI2 transcript pool responsive to pathogen challenge
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: V. dahliae infection; F. oxysporum infection; glycerol treatment
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. GhSSI2-C is the third member of the dominant GhSSI2 isoform group. 2. The group determines cotton oleic acid homeostasis. 3. Reduced group expression activates broad defense responses and improves vascular fungal resistance.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Relevant for multiplex strategies targeting all dominant SSI2 homoeologs/isoforms with careful dosage control
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Lesion mimic and defense-growth tradeoff under strong suppression
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Evidence is group-based, not fully isoform-specific.

Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: JA-responsive defense output, specifically PDF1.2-dependent response under low-18:1 conditions
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Represses or blocks JA-inducible PDF1.2 expression in the GhSSI2-silenced low-18:1 background
Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: Transcriptional repression or negative control of JA-responsive output
Regulation_Genes.Upstream_Signals_or_Inputs: Low oleic acid state caused by GhSSI2 silencing
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Expression was elevated in GhSSI2-silenced plants; cosilencing with GhSSI2 restored JA-inducible PDF1.2 expression
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: RT-qPCR; VIGS double/triple silencing; JA treatment
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. GhWRKY50 is induced in the low-18:1 GhSSI2-silenced state. 2. It contributes to blocking the PDF1.2-dependent branch of JA response. 3. Reducing GhWRKY50 together with GhSSI2 partially restores JA-inducible PDF1.2 expression.
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential modifier target to uncouple beneficial low-18:1 resistance from impaired JA-responsive outputs
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Functional inference is based on genetic interaction through VIGS; direct promoter binding was not shown.

Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: JA-responsive defense output, particularly PDF1.2-dependent signaling in low-18:1 cotton
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Negatively regulates JA-inducible PDF1.2 expression in the GhSSI2-silenced background
Regulation_Genes.Upstream_Signals_or_Inputs: Low oleic acid state due to GhSSI2 suppression
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Elevated in GhSSI2-silenced plants; cosilencing with GhSSI2 restored PDF1.2 induction by JA
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: RT-qPCR; VIGS double/triple silencing; JA treatment
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. GhWRKY51 accumulates in the GhSSI2 low-18:1 state. 2. It participates in the blockage of the PDF1.2 branch of JA signaling. 3. Combined silencing alleviates the defect in JA-induced PDF1.2 expression.
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential target for balancing disease resistance and JA responsiveness in low-oleate engineering backgrounds
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: As for GhWRKY50, direct molecular targets were inferred functionally rather than demonstrated biochemically.
